quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. Gadget was "33.43% of revenue, 12.89% more than Widget's $2,118.94" and North's share "was 53.83% above West's revenue."

specificity.why: Every recommendation names a product/region with figures, e.g. "Test Sprocket promotions: its $1,130.50 revenue was 47.26% of Gadget revenue."

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple actionable, non-obvious observations like revenue concentration in Gadget (33.43%), the North–West gap (53.83%), the median-vs-total distinction, and Sprocket's underperformance ratio to Gadget.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. Gadget is "33.43% of revenue, 12.89% more than Widget's" and North's "30.42% share was 53.83% above West's revenue".

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a specific product or region with its figure, e.g. "Test Sprocket promotions: its $1,130.50 revenue was 47.26% of Gadget revenue".

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple actionable, non-obvious observations like revenue concentration ("Gadget generated ... 33.43% of revenue"), the North–West regional gap, and Sprocket's underperformance ratio to Gadget.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. Gadget is "33.43% of revenue, 12.89% more than Widget's $2,118.94" and North's share "was 53.83% above West's revenue."

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with data, e.g. "Prioritize Gadget stock: it generated $2,392.00, 33.43% of revenue" and "Test Sprocket promotions: its $1,130.50 revenue was 47.26% of Gadget revenue."

insight_density.why: Multiple actionable observations beyond restating totals—revenue concentration (Gadget 33.43%), the regional gap (North 53.83% above West), and Sprocket lagging at 47.26% of Gadget.
